items.title: Activity selection — earliest-finish-time wins
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  S["Activities: (start, end)"]
  S --> Sort["sort by end time"]
  Sort --> Loop["pick the next activity whose start ≥ last picked end"]
  Loop --> Done["return picked set (provably max)"]
  Loop --> Loop
items.caption: Picking the activity that frees the resource soonest leaves the most room for later picks — a classic provably-optimal greedy.

items.title: When greedy fails — coin change with {1, 3, 4} and target 6
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  G["Greedy: 4 + 1 + 1 = 3 coins"]
  O["Optimal (DP): 3 + 3 = 2 coins"]
  Note["Locally biggest coin (4) blocks the better pair (3 + 3)."]
  G --- Note --- O
items.caption: When the greedy-choice property fails, local picks lock you out of the global optimum — switch to dynamic programming.
